mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, the discount rate on equities declines and valuation multiples expand, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts occur while inflation is re-accelerating and equity returns remain weak.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining over 3 months, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recent threshold indicates deteriorating labor demand and higher recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when payroll growth is also slowing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average forward return after trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 0.5 standard deviations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth combined with firmer industrial production signals broad demand acceleration, which supports corporate revenues and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is not expected when one series strengthens but the other weakens.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined-demand signal does not outperform either single proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if 20-day forward returns are not positive in the top tercile of joint RSAFS-INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in housing starts after a prior contraction signals a cyclical supply rebound and improving growth expectations, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The pattern fails if starts rise only because of volatile monthly noise without follow-through in industrial production.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% from a 6-month low
failure_condition: Reject if the post-trigger 60-day SPX return is not positive on average, or if the signal loses significance when controlling for INDPRO trend.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound, the market interprets policy as likely to stay accommodative and real rates stay suppressed, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The signal fails once inflation decelerates sharply and the same low-policy-rate setup no longer improves returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 6-month moving average and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the interaction term with inflation does not improve forecast skill over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent payroll growth above trend signals improving household income and consumption capacity, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days via stronger short-horizon risk appetite. The effect should disappear if unemployment is rising at the same time.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 0.2% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after payroll-trigger events, or if the signal becomes negative when UNRATE is flat or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies demand recession, reducing expected earnings and pushing S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when labor indicators also weaken, confirming broad economic contraction.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint contraction signal does not predict negative 60-day returns in walk-forward testing, or if adding UNRATE does not materially increase the magnitude of the negative effect.

mechanism_narrative: A weakening INDPRO trend combined with elevated unemployment signals an output gap driven by supply stress and weak cyclical momentum, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not expected to work when industrial production is improving.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE is above 6%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not underperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if the signal is positive when INDPRO has a rising 3-month slope.
